title: anemone nemorosa subsp. ranunculoides
intro: Anemone nemorosa subsp. ranunculoides, also known as wood anemone or false wood anemone, is a subspecies of the wood anemone plant, native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its delicate white flowers and three-lobed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Anemone nemorosa subsp. ranunculoides include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system. An herbal preparation of the plant can be made by infusing the roots and leaves in hot water, which can be used as a tea or applied topically as a salve.
